title: Workplace communication
explanation:
Workplace communication

  • agenda, minutes, feedback, deadline, negotiation
  • clarify, interrupt, persuade, summarise
  • phrases: make a point, raise a concern

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Please send me the meeting ____ before tomorrow morning. | *agenda | headline | contract
Could you ____ what you mean by 'urgent'? | *clarify | interrupt | negotiate
The manager gave me useful ____ on my presentation. | *feedback | broadcast | evidence
We need someone to take the meeting ____. | *minutes | platforms | vacancies

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Try not to ____ people while they are speaking. | interrupt
She managed to ____ the client to accept the proposal. | persuade
At the end, I will ____ the key decisions. | summarise
He wanted to ____ a concern about the budget. | raise
We spent two hours in ____ over the final price. | negotiation

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The project is due on Friday, so the ____ is close. | *deadline | agenda | discount
She made a strong ____ about the need for better planning. | *point | queue | forecast
I need to ____ this sentence in a simpler way. | *rephrase | delay | depart
Good teamwork depends on clear ____. | *communication | customs | recovery
